channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Uttarakhand High Court Restrains Gym Owner Deepak Kumar from Social Media Posts Amid Investigation
item.description: The Uttarakhand High Court on Friday refused to quash the case against Deepak Kumar, the gym owner who opposed a group of Bajrang Dal members for allegedly harassing an elderly Muslim shopkeeper in Pa...

item.title: Artist Creates Miniature Felted Nudibranchs Inspired by Ocean Life
item.description: Russian artist Arina Borevich is captivating audiences with her exquisite hand-felted sculptures of nudibranchs, a type of vibrantly colored sea slug. Through her project, Wool Creature Lab, Borevich ...

item.title: Bangladeshi family and friends build new Ramadan traditions in St. John's, Newfoundland
item.description: In St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, a Bangladeshi couple, Kanij and Faiaz Halim, who moved to Canada in 2023, are observing the Islamic holy month of Ramadan far from their homeland. The couple,...

item.title: Muslims Across Kenya Mark End of Ramadhan with Eid-ul-Fitr Prayers and Festivities
item.description: Across Kenya, Muslims gathered early on Friday morning, March 20, 2026, to celebrate Eid-ul-Fitr, marking the end of the holy month of Ramadhan. The day saw large congregations in mosques and public g...

item.title: Norway Crown Princess Opens Up About Friendship With Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit don talk publicly about her relationship with convicted US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein for the first time. For interview wey dem show on NRK, she admit say she was m...

item.title: Sydney father calls for stronger action on school bullying after son harassed and filmed in fight
item.description: A Sydney father is calling for justice after his 14‑year‑old son was pressured into a filmed fight in a park in the city's south‑east. The teenager says he was frightened and humiliated when other stu...

item.title: Nairobi Hospital Board and Doctors Clash Over Governance and Presidential Intervention
item.description: The Nairobi Hospital has found itself in another management saga, with doctors and the board at odds over governance and involvement of State House. According to the board, a group of doctors sought a...

item.title: Pakistan and its allies call for diplomacy to resolve Middle East tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan has spoken to the leaders of Azerbaijan, Malaysia, and Uzbekistan, urging for urgent de-escalation and a diplomatic resolution to the tensions in the Middle E...

item.title: Mumbrella Conference Faces Criticism After Murujuga UNESCO Panel Controversy
item.description: The Mumbrella 2026 CommsCon conference has found itself embroiled in a controversy over a panel discussing Murujuga, an Indigenous cultural landscape in Australia. A group of traditional owners from M...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Discusses Middle East Conflict with Leaders of Azerbaijan, Malaysia and Uzbekistan, Urges De-escalation
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Friday held telephone conversations with the leaders of Azerbaijan, Malaysia, and Uzbekistan to discuss the ongoing Middle East conflict and the need for immediate de-...

item.title: UK Government Freezes Student Loan Repayment Threshold, Increasing Burden on Plan 2 Borrowers
item.description: Recent changes to England's student loan system, announced in Chancellor Rachel Reeves' November 2025 budget, have sparked significant backlash among graduates. The core issue involves Plan 2 loans, i...

item.title: Canadian Streaming Platform Enables White Supremacist Fundraising Online
item.description: An investigation by CBC's fifth estate has revealed that a Calgary-based Canadian streaming platform called Entropy is providing white supremacists and other extremist groups a way to monetise hateful...

item.title: Proteas Women lose third T20I to New Zealand but prioritise T20 World Cup preparation
item.description: The Proteas Women's cricket team went down by six wickets to New Zealand in the third T20 International of their five-match series at Eden Park, Auckland, on Friday, 20 March 2026. Batting first, Sout...

item.title: Tanzania Ending Child Marriage Network Calls for Raising Minimum Marriage Age for Girls to 18
item.description: The Tanzania Ending Child Marriage Network (TECMN) is strongly pushing for important changes in the law so that the legal age for girls to get married becomes 18 years. Right now, under the Law of Mar...

item.title: Why big super funds are wary of members moving to SMSFs and other options
item.description: The article explains that recent government efforts to make it easier for Australians to switch superannuation funds appear to have worked, with more people than ever now changing where they hold thei...

item.title: Aremu Afolayan Apologises After Controversial Comments on Tinubu and Nigeria’s Insecurity
item.description: Actor Aremu Afolayan don come out apologise after e comments wey he make to defend President Bola Tinubu spark correct yawa online. For March 15th interview on JUJU TV, Afolayan yan say he go vote for...

item.title: TMA warns of heavy rains and possible floods in several regions on March 20–21, 2026
item.description: The Tanzania Meteorological Authority (TMA) has issued a weather alert warning of heavy rainfall across multiple regions of the country on Friday, March 20, 2026. Affected areas include Dar es Salaam,...

item.title: UK household energy bills forecast to increase by £332 annually from July due to Middle East conflict
item.description: Energy consultancy Cornwall Insight has forecasted that typical annual household energy bills in Great Britain could rise by £332 from July, reaching £1,973 for a dual-fuel household using a standard ...

item.title: RCMP Commissioner States No Current Evidence Linking Indian Government to Transnational Repression in Canada
item.description: Canadian Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) Commissioner Mike Duheme stated in an interview with CTV News that there are currently no clandestine activities or transnational repression in Canada con...

item.title: Lebanon’s Youth Suffer Deep Trauma as Israeli Bombing Continues
item.description: The ongoing Israeli bombing campaign in Lebanon has had a profound impact on the country's youth, many of whom have been displaced multiple times in just a few years. Among them is Hassan Kiki, a 16-y...
